questions.question: What is the basic idea behind lattice surgery?

options.text: Moving qubits physically across the chip
Merging and splitting code patches to perform joint measurements
Applying transversal gates to all qubits
Using classical bits to control quantum operations

questions.explanation: Lattice surgery works by merging two surface code patches to measure joint operators (like Z⊗Z or X⊗X), then splitting them back. This performs entangling operations without needing long-range physical connections.

questions.question: What does a rough merge measure between two patches A and B?
questions.explanation: A rough merge (merging along rough boundaries) measures Z_A ⊗ Z_B, the parity of the logical Z operators. The new stabilizers in the merge region have eigenvalues that reveal this joint measurement outcome.

questions.question: How many syndrome rounds does a typical lattice surgery CNOT require?
questions.explanation: A lattice surgery CNOT requires approximately 6d syndrome rounds: d for ancilla preparation, d for first merge, d for first split, d for second merge, d for second split, and d for ancilla measurement.

questions.question: Why is lattice surgery preferred over transversal CNOT for surface codes?

options.text: It only requires local 2D operations without long-range connections

questions.explanation: Lattice surgery only requires local 2D operations, which matches the connectivity constraints of real surface code hardware. Transversal CNOT would require connecting all corresponding qubit pairs, demanding either long-range connections or patch movement.

questions.question: What is the purpose of routing patches in a surface code layout?

options.text: To enable lattice surgery between non-adjacent data patches

questions.explanation: Routing patches (or ancilla patches) serve as intermediaries that allow lattice surgery operations between data patches that are not directly adjacent. They provide paths for surgical operations across the chip.

questions.question: In lattice surgery CNOT, what role does the ancilla patch play?

options.text: It mediates the CNOT through sequential merges with control and target

questions.explanation: The ancilla patch (prepared in |+_L⟩) mediates the CNOT by first merging with the control (measuring ZZ), then merging with the target (measuring XX). This sequence implements the logical CNOT operation.

questions.question: Independent lattice surgery operations on different pairs of patches can be performed in parallel.
questions.explanation: True. If two lattice surgery operations involve completely separate patches (no overlap), they can proceed simultaneously. This parallelism is important for optimizing the total computation time.
